summary: Opt-in device-resident FGMRES/GMRES-JAX routes with JAX x-block factors and device global coupling reduced GPU wall time below the timeout and avoided the earlier illegal-address failure, but both failed strict true-residual acceptance on the scale-0.60 QI hard seed.

conclusion.robustness_improvement: The new device-Krylov path converts previous timeout/illegal-address behavior into bounded nonconverged failures in about 3.6-4.2 minutes with about 4 GB host RSS.
conclusion.next_algorithmic_requirement: The remaining blocker is numerical: the QI hard seed needs a stronger true-residual preconditioner/coarse space or assembled active operator reuse, not more side-selection or timeout tuning.
